title: Metrics, thresholds and baselines deterministic AI draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, evidence-boundary fill-gap, and deterministic offline Python lab for Metrics, thresholds and baselines; all items remain blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Metrics, thresholds and baselines: reject the overclaim
items.prompt: Which statement is source-bounded and corrects this AI misconception? The model with the highest single benchmark score is best, and a metric improvement proves practical significance for every user.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Choose the statement that names mechanism, evidence conditions, and the boundary of the claim rather than a fluent or benchmark-based overgeneralization.

feedback.correct: Correct. Predeclare decision-relevant metrics and baselines, report thresholds, uncertainty, slices, and failure examples, and limit claims to the evaluated population and conditions.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the overclaim. Predeclare decision-relevant metrics and baselines, report thresholds, uncertainty, slices, and failure examples, and limit claims to the evaluated population and conditions.

criteria.description: Selects the source-bounded correction and rejects the documented AI overclaim.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against cited sources and ensure the distractor does not normalize unsafe or overstated claims.

options.text: Predeclare decision-relevant metrics and baselines, report thresholds, uncertainty, slices, and failure examples, and limit claims to the evaluated population and conditions.
The model with the highest single benchmark score is best, and a metric improvement proves practical significance for every user.

answer.explanation: Predeclare decision-relevant metrics and baselines, report thresholds, uncertainty, slices, and failure examples, and limit claims to the evaluated population and conditions.

items.title: Metrics, thresholds and baselines: canonical evidence boundary
items.prompt: Complete the exact source-bounded correction phrase; the complete sentence must remain an honest beginner-to-intermediate claim.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Retrieve the short checkpoint from the correction and ensure it narrows rather than expands the evidence claim.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'limit claims'. Predeclare decision-relevant metrics and baselines, report thresholds, uncertainty, slices, and failure examples, and limit claims to the evaluated population and conditions.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the technical and responsible-AI boundary. Predeclare decision-relevant metrics and baselines, report thresholds, uncertainty, slices, and failure examples, and limit claims to the evaluated population and conditions.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical phrase that completes the bounded source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and technically equivalent variants before learner use.
items.template: Predeclare decision-relevant metrics and baselines, report thresholds, uncertainty, slices, and failure examples, and [[checkpoint]] to the evaluated population and conditions.

answer.explanation: Predeclare decision-relevant metrics and baselines, report thresholds, uncertainty, slices, and failure examples, and limit claims to the evaluated population and conditions.

items.title: Metrics, thresholds and baselines: deterministic offline Python lab
items.prompt: Read group,true,pred binary rows. Print overall accuracy and the lowest group accuracy to three decimals.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Implement only the stated offline contract, run the visible boundary, and do not infer a real model, safety, fairness, or deployment claim from this toy result.

feedback.correct: The deterministic offline reference checks match the bounded contract; this does not establish real-world AI quality or safety.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check differs; inspect parsing, algorithm assumptions, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Passes all visible and hidden deterministic offline tests while preserving the stated evidence and non-claim boundary.

privateAuthorNotes: Offline stdlib-only task. Human reviewer must verify algorithm, fixtures, overclaim boundary, and educational validity before any exam use.

answer.explanation: The aggregate and worst slice are both bounded summaries; uncertainty and decision costs would still be required for a real claim.
